text: Hi everyone and welcome to the Java placement course. Today's lecture we are going to talk about 2D arrays. Before this video we had read about the 1D arrays. Now in 1D arrays we have given some homework problems. Their solutions are added in the same PDF. These are also the notes and homework problems. You will be getting a description box. Let's start talking about 2D arrays. Now before you know them, why is it important to read them? Now if you see only 2D arrays, they are not so complex. But if you see them from a competitive perspective, we will be able to read some complex algorithms, like dynamic programming. The twoD arrays are very useful and helpful. When we have to do some calculations, we will be able to do some complex problems. So, 2D arrays are very important. That's why we have to learn a lot from this concept. We will start the 2D arrays with a lot of basics. How to declare, how to use, how to visualize, how to get the memory. How to get the input, how to get the output, how to solve one or two questions. The basic concepts about 2D arrays are clear. Let's start with the 2D arrays in Java. Now if we talk about 2D arrays, we remember in 11-12, we used to read a structure, which was shown in the same way. We used to say this structure in the matrix. What was in the matrix? There were some rows, some columns, there were numbers in rows, like 1, 2, 3, columns, there were numbers in columns, like this, we used to store some data in the matrix, 5, 4, 39, 42, 86, 102. In some way, we used to fill the data in the box in the box. And this particular box, if we had to take out the location, we used to say this, row, column, means if it has row, then its location will be 2, 2. Because row is 2 and column is 2. So in this way, the basic concepts of the matrix which we used to use in the matrix, if you visualize this figure, then the concept of the matrix was so much. But the structure was table, the rows, columns, and we used to take out the location. Now, we took out the same structure in the matrix, and put the code in it. If we put the Java in it, then we use this matrix to say, in the code in the language of 2D arrays. So, in this way, when we get a different, different boxes collection, in a rectangle form, which has rows, columns, we use to say that we have 2D arrays. This structure is also stored in the memory, as we have used to put it in the 1D array, that we will show you something like 1D array inside the memory. Now, you put a copy down, and we will put a copy down. And if we keep it in this way, then we will have a 2D array inside the memory. So, this is the basic concept of 2D arrays. Now, how is the matrix and the coding of 2D arrays different? Because the language we are talking about in the coding, the Java, is a zero indexed base language. We read the first one, the array study time, the languages that are in the arrays, which are the indexes of the arrays, they start from zero. As in C++, the arrays start from zero. How is the matrix language that starts from 1? Now, if we start from zero, then we can know that the number 1 of the rows will be index 0. The next index will be 1, the next index will be 2. Similarly, the column number 1 index 0, 2, 1, 3, 2, and so on. Now, here, index is actually location, means that when we have to access that place in the code, then we will not write 1 for row 1, we will write 0. This is the logic of indexing. Here, if we look at the total number of rows, 3, 1, 2, 3, and total number of columns, then we will talk about 5, 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5. Similarly, this 2D array, this is the 2D array of 3 cross 5 size, where we have a single block, we have a single block, we have a whole location, we have a cell, this cell will have its size, the type of the 2D array is defined as that type of a. If you have defined the 2D array of the int type, then it will have its size, 4 bytes. If we have defined the Boolean type, then each single cell will have its size, 1 byte. Similarly, if we have defined the 2D array of the memory, the memory consumption, then we can define rows multiplied by columns, multiplied by the data type of the type of the 2D array. So, if this integer type of 2D array is the total memory consumption, then it will be the total bytes of the 2D array of the 2D array of the 2D array. The rows, columns and 4 bytes for the integer. Similarly, the basic concept of 2D array of the 2D array of the code, how we define the 2D array of the code, as we had explained in the time of the array, first we write the type, then we write the square brackets, then we write the number of the array, then we write the new keyword, int and then the actual size of the code, this was the one D array of the code.
